stats_fallback.keel_laid_coin_ceremony: 2025-03-27 (Saint-Nazaire — coin ceremony godmothers Marialuisa Iaccarino and Flavie Biondo)
stats_fallback.beam: 154 ft (47 m hull waterline); 177 ft / 54 m max including balcony overhangs
stats_fallback.power: 5× Wärtsilä 14V46DF LNG dual-fuel engines (80 MW total) + 2× 75 kW Bloom Energy Solid Oxide Fuel Cells (150 kW total) + shore power capability
stats_fallback.yacht_club_status: Scheduled from November 2026 delivery. 144 Yacht Club suites across 7 categories — the largest choice of Yacht Club suite categories in the MSC fleet as of 2026. Categories include the new Royal Duplex Suite (53 sqm, 2-deck, sleeps 6), multi-room Owner Suites, Royal Suites with whirlpool, Duplex Suites with and without whirlpool, Deluxe Suites, and Interior Suites. Over 9,900 sqm of Yacht Club space including private MSC Yacht Club Restaurant, panoramic Top Sail Lounge, Grill & Bar, bi-level private sundeck and pool, butler service, and 24-hour concierge.

related.class_notes: World Class. MSC World Asia is the third ship of four announced World Class ships (plus two additional class members ordered in November 2025 for 2030s delivery). Sisters are MSC World Europa (2022, Mediterranean-based) and MSC World America (2025, PortMiami-based). MSC World Atlantic is scheduled for 2027 delivery. All ships are built by Chantiers de l'Atlantique at Saint-Nazaire and share the class-defining Y-shaped aft with floor-to-ceiling glass walls, LNG dual-fuel propulsion, the seven-deck outdoor World Promenade, and full MSC Yacht Club enclaves. MSC World Asia differs from sisters by: (1) Asian-inspired design motifs including the suspended stainless-steel Dragon sculpture in the World Galleria and koi-carp artworks; (2) The Spiral @ Tree of Life — a 266.7 ft (81 m) 12-deck dry slide, one deck taller than Europa's and America's 11-deck Venom Drop @ The Spiral, and MSC's longest dry slide at sea as of 2026; (3) a new-to-class Pan-Asian Street Food specialty restaurant; (4) the redesigned Harbour family park with the Cliffhanger 164-ft over-water swing, Sky Rails high-ropes course, aquapark, Harbour Bar & Bites and Play Deck; (5) The Clubhouse retro family venue with bumper cars, roller skating, basketball, table games, and a LEGO Family Zone; (6) Manila Bay primary outdoor pool with Pearl Lagoon Bar and Coral Cove indoor pool with retractable roof and a water-droplet art installation by Italian sculptor Elisabetta Milan; (7) 144 Yacht Club suites across 7 categories — the largest choice of Yacht Club suite categories in the MSC fleet — including the new Royal Duplex Suite (53 sqm, 2-deck, sleeps 6); (8) MSC's first all-American-style sports bar sailing in Europe (All-Stars Sports Bar); and (9) Mediterranean deployment from Barcelona rather than Asia-Pacific despite her name.

deployment.maiden_season_2026_27: Year-round 7-night Western Mediterranean round-trips from Barcelona starting with the maiden voyage on 4 December 2026. Homeports: Barcelona, Marseille, Genoa, Civitavecchia (Rome), Naples, Messina, Valletta. Despite the name, World Asia deploys in the Mediterranean — not Asia-Pacific.
deployment.typical_itinerary: Barcelona → Marseille → Genoa → Civitavecchia (Rome) → Naples → Messina (Sicily) → Valletta (Malta) → Barcelona
deployment.current_status_2026_04_11: In final fit-out at Chantiers de l'Atlantique, Saint-Nazaire, France. Floated out 7 November 2025; sea trials expected mid-to-late 2026 ahead of November 2026 delivery.

history.steel_cutting: 9 April 2024 at a ceremony held in Miami Beach, Florida (highlighting MSC's North American strategy)
history.keel_laid_coin_ceremony: 27 March 2025 at Chantiers de l'Atlantique, Saint-Nazaire — coin-ceremony godmothers: Marialuisa Iaccarino (MSC Cruises) and Flavie Biondo (MSC Cruises)
history.march_2026_update: 25 March 2026 — MSC Cruises revealed revamped Family and Pool District details, confirming interior fit-out was progressing on schedule
history.scheduled_maiden_voyage: 4 December 2026 — 7-night Western Mediterranean round-trip from Barcelona

discrepancies_flagged.imo_contamination_10th_caught: Previous page had IMO 9895081 — which does not match MSC World Asia in any AIS database. Correct IMO is 9901556 per VesselFinder, CruiseMapper, and Marine Vessel Traffic. This is the TENTH IMO cross-contamination caught across the MSC fleet repair (after Bellissima, Euribia, Magnifica, Meraviglia, Musica/Orchestra swap, Seascape-vs-tug-LUBRANI, Seaside/Seaview sister-pair swap, Sinfonia, and World Europa).
discrepancies_flagged.deployment_wrong: Previous page claimed MSC World Asia is 'designed for Asian deployments' with 'Asia-Pacific' itineraries. This is wrong. Despite the name, MSC World Asia deploys in the Western Mediterranean — Barcelona homeport with a 4 December 2026 maiden voyage. Verified via MSC press release October 2025, Seatrade Cruise, and Cruise Industry News.
discrepancies_flagged.class_placeholder_removed: Previous page had 'exceptional' as placeholder class name in multiple places (intro, Review JSON-LD, CruiseShip JSON-LD, Article JSON-LD, key facts, stats fallback, stats card, Quick Answer block). Fixed throughout to 'World Class'.
discrepancies_flagged.cirque_hallucination_removed: Previous Review JSON-LD claimed 'Cirque du Soleil at Sea' on MSC World Asia. This is wrong — the entire MSC × Cirque du Soleil at Sea partnership has been phased out, and World Asia was never part of it. Removed from Review JSON-LD and replaced with MSC's in-house production shows plus The Loft, Luna Park Arena, and All-Stars Sports Bar.
discrepancies_flagged.guest_count_hallucinations: Previous page had '6,762 guests at double occupancy' and '5,264 guests' in different places. Verified: 5,079 at double occupancy / 6,762 maximum per CruiseMapper, Cruise Critic, and Travelextra (with minor source conflicts on max: 6,758-6,782; 6,762 is MSC's press figure consistent with sisters).
discrepancies_flagged.crew_count_wrong: Previous stats card listed crew as '2,200'. Correct is 2,138 per CruiseMapper.
discrepancies_flagged.meraviglia_sister_image: Previous first-look carousel had an MSC Meraviglia exterior photo captioned as 'Fleet sister MSC Meraviglia'. MSC Meraviglia is NOT a sister of MSC World Asia — she is the lead ship of the older Meraviglia class (2017), not the World Class. Removed.
discrepancies_flagged.under_construction_false_positive: The page references World Asia's delivery (November 2026) which is in the future from today's date (April 2026). This is accurate, not construction-banner content; the page uses 'scheduled November 2026 delivery' phrasing rather than 'currently under construction'.
discrepancies_flagged.godmothers_coin_ceremony_added: Previous page did not identify the coin-ceremony godmothers. Added: Marialuisa Iaccarino and Flavie Biondo (MSC Cruises), per MSC press release March 2025. The christening godmother (at delivery in November 2026) has not yet been announced by MSC.
discrepancies_flagged.yacht_club_144_suites: World Europa has 152 and World America has 159 Yacht Club suites, but World Asia has FEWER at 144 (in 7 categories). MSC's press phrase 'biggest choice of Yacht Club suites' refers to the largest number of suite CATEGORIES (7), not the largest count. Verified via MSC press release October 2025 and Cruise Industry News.
discrepancies_flagged.beam: 47 m hull waterline vs 54 m max with balcony overhangs. AIS trackers report 54 m because AIS reports maximum beam. MSC press releases quote 47 m hull. Both are correct for different measurement points.
discrepancies_flagged.wrong_world_galleria_6_deck_claim: Previous page mentioned the 'outdoor promenade spanning six decks'. The World Promenade is SEVEN decks (class-wide spec), not six. Fixed.
